facts.text: Salem’s infamous witch trials began in 1692, ending with 19 executions by hanging and the dramatic death of Giles Corey, who was pressed to death for refusing to plead.
The Salem Ferry, named after navigator Nathaniel Bowditch, has been rebuilt with a new 350‑foot pier completed in 2011 to replace the old trailer dock.
Pioneer Village, a living‑history museum, served as a filming location for the supernatural series Castle Rock, showcasing Salem’s pilgrim‑era streets.
Samuel McIntire’s Federal‑style mansions, designed for early American elites, give Salem’s largest historic district a distinctive architectural character.
Salem State University, founded in 1854, hosts an observatory, an art gallery, and the 50‑sequence mural The Chronicle of Salem.
The Friendship of Salem, a 171‑foot replica of an 18th‑century East Indiaman, sails from Salem’s historic waterfront, echoing its maritime past.
